title: ceanothus divergens subsp. occidentalis
intro: Ceanothus divergens subsp. occidentalis, also known as deerbrush, is a shrub native to western North America, characterized by its dense, fragrant foliage and vibrant blue flowers. The medicinal properties of deerbrush include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to promote respiratory health, boost the immune system, and support skin and wound healing, offering health benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved cough relief. Deerbrush can be prepared as a tea, infused oil, or tincture for its medicinal and therapeutic uses.
